boundaryZeroInputStatus.label: primitive boundary correction is zero
boundaryZeroInputStatus.reason: The primitive boundary-zero consequence gives D_bdy=0.

dqZeroInputStatus.label: quotient repair vanishes on X_R
dqZeroInputStatus.reason: The Green-feature theorem gives D_q=0 on the completed trace range.

dqVanishesOnXRStatus.label: quotient repair D_q vanishes on X_R
dqVanishesOnXRStatus.reason: This is the exact trace-side consequence consumed by the canonical boundary repair comparison: D_q=0 on the completed transported trace range X_R.

primitiveDensityInputStatus.label: D_q zero transfers to primitive trace image
primitiveDensityInputStatus.reason: The primitive trace image is dense in X_R and D_q is bounded.

primitiveEndpointCompatibilityStatus.label: primitive endpoint compatibility
primitiveEndpointCompatibilityStatus.reason: The primitive boundary correction and quotient repair both vanish on primitive traces.

proof: D_q=0 on X_R by the Green-feature contraction.
Primitive trace density transfers D_q=0 to primitive traces.
Therefore endpoint/trace correction is annihilated for the original primitive lift.
